title: बुध स्तोत्र
devanagariText:
॥ श्री बुध स्तोत्रम् ॥

बुधं प्रणम्य मनसा वचसा च भावतः।
सर्वज्ञं सर्वगं देवं सर्वलोकनमस्कृतम्॥ १॥

प्रियङ्गुकलिकाश्यामं रूपेणाप्रतिमं बुधम्।
सौम्यं सौम्यगुणोपेतं तं बुधं प्रणमाम्यहम्॥ २॥

सोमसूनुं सोमवक्त्रं सोमधर्मानुवर्तिनम्।
सुखदं सुभगं शान्तं तं बुधं प्रणमाम्यहम्॥ ३॥

चतुर्भुजं पीतवर्णं खड्गचर्मधरं प्रभुम्।
सिंहवाहनमासीनं तं बुधं प्रणमाम्यहम्॥ ४॥

वाणिज्यकर्मसन्तुष्टं वाक्पटुं शास्त्रपारगम्।
गणितज्ञानसम्पन्नं तं बुधं प्रणमाम्यहम्॥ ५॥

विद्यावान् वेदवेद्यश्च वाक्पतिश्च महाबलः।
बुद्धिदो बुद्धिमान् बुद्धो बुद्धिशास्त्रविशारदः॥ ६॥

सिंहाधिरूढो वरदो बुधो बुद्धिमतां वरः।
विष्णुरूपो बुधः श्रेष्ठः सर्वज्ञो वाक्पतिः प्रभुः॥ ७॥

ज्ञानदो ज्ञानकर्ता च ज्ञानज्ञेयविवर्धनः।
मतिमान् मतिदातृश्च मतिमद्भिर्नमस्कृतः॥ ८॥

॥ फलश्रुति ॥
बुधस्तोत्रमिदं पुण्यं सर्वपापप्रणाशनम्।
सर्वकामप्रदं नृणां बुद्धिवर्धनकारकम्॥

यः पठेत् बुधवारे तु बुधदोषनिवारणम्।
बुधग्रहशमं याति बुधनामजपात् सदा॥

॥ इति श्री बुध स्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Budh Stotram ||

Budham pranamya manasa vachasa cha bhavatah |
Sarvajnam sarvagam devam sarvaloka-namaskritam || 1 ||

Priyanguka-likashyamam rupenapratimam Budham |
Saumyam saumya-gunopetam tam Budham pranamyaham || 2 ||

Somasunum Somavaktram Soma-dharma-anuvartinal |
Sukhadam subhagam shantam tam Budham pranamyaham || 3 ||

Chaturbhujam pitavarnam khadga-charma-dharam prabhum |
Simha-vahanam-asinam tam Budham pranamyaham || 4 ||

Vanijya-karma-santushtam vakpatum shastra-paragam |
Ganita-jnana-sampannam tam Budham pranamyaham || 5 ||

Vidyavan Vedavedyash-cha Vakpatish-cha mahabalah |
Buddhido buddhiman Buddho buddhi-shastra-visharadah || 6 ||

Simhadhirudho varado Budho buddhimatam varah |
Vishnurupo Budhah shreshthah sarvajno Vakpatih prabhuh || 7 ||

Jnanado jnanakarta cha jnana-jneya-vivardhanah |
Matiman matidatrish-cha matimadbhir-namaskritah || 8 ||

|| Phalashruti ||
Budh-stotram-idam punyam sarva-papa-pranashanam |
Sarva-kama-pradam nrinam buddhi-vardhana-karakam ||

Yah pathet Budhavare tu Budha-dosha-nivaranam |
Budha-graha-shamam yati Budha-nama-japat sada ||

|| Iti Shri Budh Stotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
मन, वचन और भाव से बुध को प्रणाम करता हूँ, जो सर्वज्ञ, सर्वव्यापी देव, सम्पूर्ण लोकों द्वारा नमस्कृत हैं॥ १॥

प्रियंगु की कली के समान श्यामवर्ण, अप्रतिम रूप वाले, सौम्य, सौम्य गुणों से युक्त बुध को मैं प्रणाम करता हूँ॥ २॥

सोम (चन्द्रमा) के पुत्र, सोम के समान मुख वाले, सोम के धर्म का पालन करने वाले, सुख देने वाले, सुभग, शान्त बुध को मैं प्रणाम करता हूँ॥ ३॥

चतुर्भुज, पीतवर्ण, खड्ग और ढाल धारी, सिंह वाहन पर बैठे प्रभु बुध को मैं प्रणाम करता हूँ॥ ४॥

वाणिज्य कर्म में सन्तुष्ट, वाक्पटु, शास्त्रों में पारंगत, गणित ज्ञान से सम्पन्न बुध को मैं प्रणाम करता हूँ॥ ५॥

ज्ञान देने वाले, ज्ञान की रचना करने वाले, ज्ञान और ज्ञेय की वृद्धि करने वाले, मतिमान, मति देने वाले, बुद्धिमानों द्वारा नमस्कृत हैं॥ ८॥

यह पवित्र बुध स्तोत्र सम्पूर्ण पापों का नाश करने वाला, सम्पूर्ण कामनाएँ पूर्ण करने वाला और बुद्धि की वृद्धि करने वाला है। जो बुधवार को पाठ करता है, उसके बुध दोष दूर होते हैं॥
benefits: Traditionally recited for removal of afflictions of planet mercury (budha)
Invokes sharp intellect, eloquence, and communication skills
Invokes the blessing of success in commerce, trade, and business
Traditionally recited for removal of speech defects and communication problems
Invokes wisdom, learning, and academic success
Especially effective when chanted on Wednesdays
description: Budh Stotra (बुध स्तोत्र) is a sacred prayer dedicated to the Nine Planets (Navagraha). Rooted in Skanda Purana, this composition carries deep spiritual significance. Regular recitation is believed to remove afflictions of planet Mercury (Budha), remove speech defects and communication problems, and grant sharp intellect, eloquence, and communication skills. This prayer is traditionally recited on Wednesday (Budhvar) to invoke divine grace. With 8 verses and a reading time of about 5 minutes, this prayer is ideal for both daily worship and special occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Budh Stotra in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Budh Stotra in Sanskrit with transliteration and Hindi meaning. Prayer to planet Mercury for intellect, speech, and removing Budh dosha.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Budh Stotra and where is it traditionally recorded?
seoFaqs.answer: The Budh Stotra is a short Sanskrit hymn of eight to nine shlokas dedicated to Budha, the planetary deity of Mercury, who is described in the puranic tradition as the son of Chandra (the Moon) and Tara. The most widely chanted version, beginning Priyangu kalika shyamam rupena-apratimam Budham, is preserved in the Skanda Purana within its larger Navagraha stotra cycle and is also reproduced in the Brahma Purana and several jyotisha digests. It is a core remedial recitation prescribed by traditional astrologers for any horoscope where Budha is afflicted, weak, combust or placed in malefic dasha. (Sources: Stotra Nidhi; Vedadhara; Drikpanchang Navagraha.)

seoFaqs.question: What does Budha look like according to the dhyana shloka of the Budh Stotra?
seoFaqs.answer: The Budh Stotra describes Budha as a youthful four-armed deity with a complexion the dark green of priyangu flower buds, seated upon a lion or in some traditions a chariot drawn by lions. He holds a sword (khadga), a shield (charma), a mace (gada) and shows the boon-granting varada mudra. He wears yellow or green silk garments, a golden crown and is adorned with emeralds, the gemstone associated with Mercury. This iconography links him to intelligence, eloquence and commerce — the karakatva of the planet Mercury in Vedic astrology. (Sources: Stotra Nidhi Budha Stotram; Vedadhara.)

seoFaqs.question: When should Budh Stotra be chanted for best results?
seoFaqs.answer: Traditional almanacs prescribe Wednesday (Budhvar), the day ruled by Mercury, especially during the early morning hora of Budha. Devotees facing Budha mahadasha or antardasha, Budha-Ketu or Budha-Rahu combinations, or weak Mercury are advised to recite it eleven, twenty-one or one hundred and eight times on Wednesdays starting from a Shukla Paksha Wednesday. It is also chanted during the annual Navagraha homa, on Budha Pradosha days, and as part of daily nitya-sandhya for those installed in jyotisha sadhana. (Sources: Drikpanchang Wednesday remedies; AstroSage Budha mahadasha; Vedadhara.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the phalashruti of Budh Stotra promise?
seoFaqs.answer: The closing phalashruti states that the hymn destroys all sins (sarva-papa-pranashanam), fulfils all desires (sarva-kama-pradam) and increases intellect (buddhi-vardhana-karakam). Reciting it on Wednesday is specifically said to remove Budha-dosha and pacify the malefic effects of Mercury (Budha-graha-shamam). Astrological tradition extends these to success in trade and commerce, eloquence in speech, mathematical and analytical sharpness, removal of speech defects such as stammering, success in examinations, and protection from skin and nervous-system disorders attributed to an afflicted Mercury. (Sources: Stotra Nidhi Budha Stotram; AstroSage Budh remedies.)

seoFaqs.question: How is the Budh Stotra connected to the Navagraha worship system?
seoFaqs.answer: Budha is the fourth of the nine planetary deities (navagraha) worshipped in temple pradakshina. In the Navagraha stotra cycle each of the nine planets has a short stuti recited in sequence — Surya, Chandra, Mangala, Budha, Guru, Shukra, Shani, Rahu, Ketu — and the Budh Stotra is the fourth stuti in this sequence. In major Navagraha kshetras such as Tiruvenkadu in Tamil Nadu, where Budha is the main planetary lord, this stotra is chanted during the deity's archana along with green moong-dal naivedyam and the offering of emeralds. (Sources: Drikpanchang Navagraha; Tiruvenkadu temple guides.)

seoFaqs.question: What gemstones, mantras and offerings accompany Budh Stotra recitation?
seoFaqs.answer: Traditional jyotisha pairs the Budh Stotra with the Budha beej mantra Om Bram Brim Braum Sah Budhaya Namah, japa with an emerald or jade mala, and naivedyam of green moong-dal khichdi and bottle gourd. The recommended dana on Wednesdays includes green cloth, emeralds, bronze utensils, sugarcane and books donated to students. Wearing an emerald set in gold on the little finger after vedic prana-pratishtha is the prescribed talismanic remedy when the stotra is chanted at the time of installation. (Sources: AstroSage Budha remedies; Drikpanchang Wednesday vrat.)

seoFaqs.question: How is Budh Stotra different from Budha Kavacham and Budha Sahasranama?
seoFaqs.answer: All three texts are Mercury-pacification recitations but serve different ritual purposes. Budh Stotra is the brief eight-verse praise hymn used for daily Wednesday recitation. Budha Kavacham is the longer protective armour-prayer that envelopes each limb of the chanter in Budha's protective grace, recited when Mercury is in heavy transit affliction or during Budha mahadasha onset. Budha Sahasranama lists one thousand divine epithets of Mercury and is used for elaborate planetary homas and ashtottara archana in temples. The Stotra is the most accessible of the three and is the recommended starting point for householders. (Sources: Stotra Nidhi; Vedadhara Budha texts.)
